text:
Powered byOutside

(Photo: DIMITAR DILKOFF/AFP via Getty Images)

Elouan Gardon (Acme, Washington) powered to bronze forTeam USAfor the first U.S. cycling medal in theParis 2024 Paralympic Games.



Competing in the men’s 4000-meter C5 individual pursuit, the 18-year-old narrowly missed gold medal contention, but secured a spot in the bronze medal final.

He roared to bronze six seconds ahead of Austria’s Franz-Josef Lasser.

“It feels pretty surreal,” Gardon said in a Team USA release. “I am so incredibly happy, and happy to get the first medal for Para-cycling in the Paralympic Games. I can’t wait to continue.

“I’ve never had a crowd like this before,” Gardon said. “The feeling is absolutely exciting.”

The youngest member of@usparacyclingtakes home BRONZE in his Paralympic debut!Incredible performance from Elouan Gardon!#ParisParalympicspic.twitter.com/dJDaCeBMOI



Veteran teammate Bryan Larsen (Windsor, California) spotted Gardon’s talent as a road rider and encouraged him to pursuepara-cycling.

After receiving an invitation to a talent identification camp in June, it became evident that Gardon possessed untapped potential, officials said.

Larsen also took to the track, competing in the men’s 4000m C4 individual pursuit race opposite Team France, where the rowdy home crowds did not rattle Larsen.

“It was almost numbing to a certain degree, it was almost better,” Larsen said. “Because it was so loud, it almost drowned my own thoughts, which sometimes negative thoughts can creep in. I was really, really happy to be able to subconsciously stick to my habits.”

Larsen beat his own national record by nearly a full second, a time he had previously set in March at the 2024 UCI Para-Cycling World Championships, but that was not enough to reach the medal round.
title: Elouan Gardon Delivers First Para-cycling Medal for Team USA
author: Andrew Hood
description: The Olympic rookie roared to bronze six seconds ahead of Austria’s Franz-Josef Lasser: 'It feels pretty surreal.'
